baseline.reason: Correctly identifies obj_string_stream chain corruption (line 818) as root cause and names the bit_read_BB sink, but the trigger path is wrong: it references bit_read_H instead of bit_read_CMC, conflates `dat` with `str_dat`, and misses the key fix (str->byte/str->size restoration and obj->has_strings guard).

vastdb.reason: Misses the core obj_string_stream state corruption entirely. Focuses generically on bit_advance_position overflow handling (line 787-802) which is only a minor contributor; the trigger path (bit_read_BB -> bit_advance_position) omits the actual chain-pointer corruption path through obj_string_stream and bit_read_CMC.
